name: Dr Kathleen Orndahl
school: School of Informatics, Computing, and Cyber Systems
focus: Arctic vegetation ecology • UAV and satellite remote sensing • Caribou ecology
bio: Kathleen Orndahl is a Senior Research Scientist at Northern Arizona University, affiliated with the School of Informatics, Computing, and Cyber Systems. Her research focuses on Arctic vegetation ecology and how climate change is reshaping high-latitude ecosystems, integrating field measurements, UAV operations, and satellite remote sensing to map plant biomass, woody dominance, and habitat dynamics across the tundra biome. Her recent work includes leading the development of next-generation Arctic vegetation maps that combine field biomass harvest measurements, Landsat data, and machine learning to produce 30 m-resolution maps of aboveground plant biomass and woody dominance across the Arctic tundra (Remote Sensing of Environment, 2025), and contributing to the Arctic Plant Aboveground Biomass Synthesis Dataset (Scientific Data, 2024). She also studies caribou ecology, and her work has direct applications in vegetation monitoring, wildlife conservation, carbon accounting, and land surface modeling.
